qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Console command injection allows arbitrary command execution on game server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns the Panel console command endpoint forwarding unsanitized command strings to Wings; the advisory concerns Wings websocket endpoints lacking rate limits, throttling, and message-size limits that enable resource-exhaustion DoS.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in File Pull Feature
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns the Panel file-pull URL validation path and SSRF to internal destinations; the advisory concerns Wings websocket endpoints lacking rate limits, throttling, and message-size limits that enable resource-exhaustion DoS.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Wings websocket resource-exhaustion root cause. The target advisory is about missing websocket rate limits, throttling, and message-size limits in github.com/pterodactyl/wings, while the qualifying findings are unrelated Panel API issues: console command injection and file-pull SSRF.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-8w7m-w749-rx98 describes websocket endpoints in Wings allowing excessive connections and oversized websocket messages that overload network, CPU, and memory.
console-command-injection reports POST /api/client/servers/{server}/command, CommandController.php, DaemonCommandRepository.php, and SendCommandRequest.php as the vulnerable path.
ssrf-file-pull reports POST /api/client/servers/{server}/files/pull, FileController.php, PullFileRequest.php, and DaemonFileRepository.php as the vulnerable path.
Neither qualifying report identifies websocket connection/message handling, rate limiting, throttling, message-size enforcement, or resource-exhaustion behavior in Wings.
